channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Paul Manafort: Peace is not in Zelenskyy's interest
item.description: Manafort, who advised former Ukrainian President Viktor Yanukovych, said Zelenskyy has political motivations for prolonging the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pauses US military aid to Ukraine while pressuring Zelenskyy to move toward quick end to war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday directed a “pause” to U.S. assistance to Ukraine as he seeks to pressure Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to engage in negotiations to end the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump says 25% tariffs on Mexican and Canadian imports will start Tuesday, with 'no room' for delay
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that 25% taxes on imports from Mexico and Canada would start Tuesday, sparking renewed fears of a North American trade war that already showed signs of pushing up inflation and hindering growth. “Tomorrow — tariffs 25% on Canada and 25% on Mexico.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ZEKE MILLER and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Palestinians hope 'No Other Land' Oscar win brings help as they face possible Israeli expulsion
item.description: AL-TUWANEH, West Bank (AP) — Just last week, Israeli troops came and tore down a Palestinian family’s shed in this remote, hilly corner of the West Bank, residents say. It was the latest instance of destruction targeting a collection of hamlets whose population is threatened with expulsion.
item.dc:creator: SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Head of FBI New York office says he's retired from the bureau after being ordered to do so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of the FBI's New York field office, who was reported to have resisted Justice Department efforts to scrutinize agents who participated in politically sensitive investigations, has told coworkers that he has retired from the bureau after being directed to do so.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Trump's history with Russia and Ukraine set the stage for a blowup with Zelenskyy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As his White House meeting with his Ukrainian counterpart devolved into a stunning blowup, President Donald Trump leaned on a familiar refrain to explain his unique kinship with Russian leader Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Universal Studios Orlando teases new attractions across its theme parks
item.description: Universal Studios Orlando teased that new projects for all its theme parks are in the works. In a video promoting its latest theme park, Epic Universe, Karen Irwin, president and COO of Universal Studios Orlando, hinted that more projects are in the works.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department offers staff $25K incentive to quit before Monday deadline
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education is giving the majority of its staff a Monday deadline to accept a buyout of up to $25,000 ahead of reports of a 'very significant' reduction in force.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: Cat food recalled over risk of bird flu weeks after pets euthanized
item.description: The Wild Coast LLC product 16oz and 24oz frozen Boneless Free Range Chicken Formula raw pet food for cats was distributed to pet food retailers in Washington State and Oregon. The product is frozen in a small round white plastic container with a green label.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, Fox 5

item.title: Couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 men charged, nitrous oxide seized after investigation in Nashville
item.description: Officers reportedly seized more than 51,000 grams of nitrous oxide in cans, drug paraphernalia, marijuana and several hundred dollars in cash.
item.dc:creator: Aubriella Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance's role in Zelenskyy clash puts spotlight on VP
item.description: Vice President JD Vance's vocal involvement was among several noteworthy moments and led to mixed reactions.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Today in history: 'Star-Spangled Banner' made official national anthem
item.description: It’s sung at athletic and political events alike — and Monday, "The Star-Spangled Banner” marks 94 years of being America’s national anthem.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oscars 2025: Michelle Trachtenberg, Shannen Doherty, more left out of In Memoriam segment
item.description: As is nearly always the case, a few famous faces were missing from the In Memoriam segment — and their fans were quick to notice.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Youngkin grants clemency to a fired Virginia police sergeant who shot and killed an unarmed man
item.description: Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin has granted clemency to a fired police sergeant who shot and killed an unarmed man accused of stealing sunglasses.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at 97th Academy Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Inside Trump’s federal worker firings
item.description: Some of the removals have targeted a series of officials sure to conduct oversight of Trump’s actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: 175 wildfires in North and South Carolina force some evacuations
item.description: Wildfires in North and South Carolina have scorched thousands of acres as crews battling more than 175 blazes face dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Enabling this phone setting reduces anxiety, Gen Z says
item.description: It can create "a buffer against unwanted communications and potential social pressures," a psychotherapist said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Firefighters working to contain wildfires in North, South Carolina
item.description: Crews have been battling wildfires in North and South Carolina this weekend, competing with dry conditions and gusty winds.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to watch
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It's almost time to see how the biggest nailbiter Oscar season of recent years concludes. Stars will converge at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood on Sunday for the 97th annual Academy Awards, which will undoubtedly see some first-time Oscar winners in top categories.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lee Kunzman, 'persistent' Indy 500 veteran, dies at 80
item.description: Kunzman's best finish in his INDYCAR SERIES career was in 1979, when he placed second at Atlanta Motor Speedway. It was just a narrow loss to winner Johnny Rutherford, IMS officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: Utah cheerleaders among evacuees after 'incident' at national competition in Texas
item.description: Utah cheerleaders and their family members were among the people evacuated from a Dallas, Texas, convention center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WGN 9
